preconditions: v0.1.28 pressure tank rack slice committed and pushed
v0.1.28 release notes refreshed so candidate readiness no longer has a pending release-notes state
Main lane starts from a clean baseline before applying v0.1.29 changes

recommendation.primary: Convert accepted manual route-triage notes into tracked v0.1.29 work and fix the highest-impact P0/P1 route issue if one exists.
recommendation.fallback_if_no_route_issue: Perform Windows release-distribution hardening by adding a human-friendly package release index and SHA-256 verification instructions to the packaged build and candidate-readiness evidence.
recommendation.route_safe_reason: The fallback improves the Windows distributable without changing scene geometry, collision, enemy placement, combat balance, or route distances immediately after the pressure tank rack promotion.

acceptance_gates: No accepted P0/P1 manual issue remains untracked after triage.
Route audit reports no route-blocking scene composition issues.
Full V0 build matrix passes after any code, scene, package-tooling, or generated-package change.
Candidate-readiness evidence is refreshed for v0.1.29.
Fallback package files are present in both the package folder and ZIP when the no-issue fallback is used.
verification_commands: rg -n "Issue ID:|Status: new|Status: accepted|Severity: P0|Severity: P1|blocked|confusing|unreadable" Documentation/QA/ManualPlaytestV1 Documentation/QA/WindowsRouteQA
risks: Automated route audit does not replace human feel/playability review.
v0.1.28 candidate readiness showed release notes pending when reviewed by this sidecar, then the main lane refreshed readiness before commit.
Adding another prop immediately after pressure tank rack could hide route readability regressions.
Distribution files must be checked inside the ZIP, not just in the staging folder.
write_scope_compliance: This sidecar packet only writes under Documentation/Planning/V0_1_29_RouteTriage/.
